explanations.5: The ${2:-\033[1;32m} syntax means 'use the second argument if it was passed in, otherwise use this default ANSI color code for bold green'.
explanations.9: The ${#cmd_str} syntax gets the length (number of characters) of the cmd_str variable, and -gt means 'greater than'.
explanations.10: The ${cmd_str:0:197} syntax takes a substring starting at position 0 and grabs the first 197 characters, then we append '...' to show it was cut off.
explanations.13: The -e flag tells echo to interpret ANSI escape sequences (like the color codes) so they actually render as colors instead of printing the code itself.
